message:
bench(core): asupersync structured task spawn latency benchmarks (wa-1bznu)

Adds Criterion benchmarks comparing asupersync's structured task
spawning patterns against equivalent raw tokio spawn/join workloads.

Benchmarked scenarios (each at fanout sizes 1/10/100/1000):
- scope_spawn_noop: single asupersync spawn+join of a no-op task
- tokio_spawn_noop: equivalent tokio::spawn baseline
- bounded_fanout: spawn_bounded_with_cx fan-out with Cx propagation
- flat_fanout: spawn_with_cx fan-out (unbounded)
- tokio_flat_fanout: raw tokio::spawn fan-out baseline

Feature-gated behind asupersync-runtime to avoid pulling in heavy
deps for default builds. Uses bench_common module for budget
metadata and JSONL artifact emission.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","title":"Build cross-project extraction matrix (frankensqlite vs cass vs xf)","description":"## Goal
Synthesize findings from frankensqlite/cass/xf into a single adaptation matrix that directly drives implementation sequencing.

## Background
Separate studies are not enough; we need a unified decision artifact that translates research into implementation-ready work packages.

## Scope
- Build a matrix by subsystem: ingest/write durability/indexing/query/ranking/ops.
- For each subsystem: identify source project precedent, adaptation cost, risk, and test implications.
- Produce explicit recommendations for FrankenTerm target architecture.

## Deliverables
- Architecture extraction matrix (self-contained and implementation-oriented).
- Traceability table linking each recommendation to downstream beads.

## Acceptance criteria
- Matrix includes risk/complexity scores and dependency hints.
- Downstream track owners can implement without revisiting source repos.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.598965Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:12.429143Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:12.429129Z","close_reason":"Cross-project extraction matrix delivered and mapped to downstream beads","labels":["architecture","planning","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.598965Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.6938Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.723532Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.752861Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed cross-project synthesis matrix and saved deliverable to docs/flight-recorder/cross-project-extraction-matrix.md. Matrix merges frankensqlite durability patterns, cass search architecture patterns, and xf operational patterns into implementation-default decisions and sequencing mapped to ft-oegrb tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","title":"Author ADR: target flight-recorder architecture + phased implementation map","description":"## Goal
Write an ADR for the target flight-recorder + hybrid-search architecture and lock first implementation increment boundaries.

## Background
A formal decision record reduces churn and prevents drift once implementation starts across multiple agents.

## Scope
- Define canonical architecture for v1 rollout.
- Define phased implementation sequence (MVP -> hardened -> scale).
- Define explicit non-goals, deferrals, and rollback boundaries.
- Define success metrics and gate criteria per phase.

## Deliverables
- ADR with decision rationale and alternatives considered.
- Sequenced execution map and handoff checklist for implementation agents.

## Acceptance criteria
- ADR is self-contained and references only stable bead artifacts.
- All implementation tracks have explicit entry conditions and dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.631865Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:55:00.093104Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:55:00.09309Z","close_reason":"ADR and phased implementation map delivered","labels":["adr","architecture","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.631865Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.782153Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Published ADR + phased implementation map at docs/flight-recorder/adr-0001-flight-recorder-architecture.md. Decision set includes four-plane architecture, canonical append-log source-of-truth, lexical-first + hybrid expansion strategy, governance gates, and phase-based rollout criteria mapped to ft-oegrb tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T07:54:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","title":"Run feasibility spikes for writer/indexer/hybrid rank hot paths","description":"## Goal
Execute focused technical spikes that validate the highest-risk assumptions before full implementation begins.

## Background
Research and ADR reduce uncertainty, but spikes are needed to validate hot-path feasibility and integration complexity.

## Scope
- Spike append-only write throughput envelope with representative event volume.
- Spike Tantivy incremental indexing from append log snapshots.
- Spike hybrid query fusion behavior on a small truth-set.

## Deliverables
- Spike report with measured metrics and pass/fail against target budgets.
- Updated risk register and recommended sequencing adjustments.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one spike per risk category (write path, indexing, ranking).
- Failures convert into explicit blocker beads before implementation proceeds.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:01:22.431053Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:01:22.431039Z","close_reason":"Feasibility spikes executed with results and thresholds documented","labels":["performance","search","spike"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.811381Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.1.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Ran feasibility spikes and published report at docs/flight-recorder/feasibility-spike-results.md. Included: synthetic append-write throughput tests, cass Tantivy criterion proxy metrics (index_small_batch/search_latency), and hybrid RRF fusion scaling microbench. Conclusions and implementation threshold recommendations are documented for downstream tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:01:15Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-oegrb.2","title":"Capture track: canonical ingress/egress event schema + mux tap semantics","description":"## Track objective
Define and implement the canonical flight-recorder event model and capture tap points for **all mux ingress/egress text** with deterministic ordering metadata.

## Why this track exists
If capture semantics are ambiguous, durability and search become untrustworthy. This track establishes the contract for what is captured, in what order, and with what context.

## Deliverables
- Event schema (ingress/egress/control markers/metadata)
- Tap-point implementation plan for mux boundaries
- Causality/sequence model across panes and workflows
- Backpressure/failure semantics for capture stage

## Acceptance criteria
- Event schema is versioned, testable, and replay-compatible.
- Capture contract is explicit enough for independent indexing/search teams to implement against it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645175Z","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

## Acceptance criteria

- Capture contract is explicit enough for independent indexing/search teams to implement against it.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PinkMoose","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:27:06.457606Z","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","title":"Define versioned recorder event schema for ingress/egress mux text","description":"## Goal
Define a versioned canonical event schema for mux ingress/egress text capture, including metadata needed for replay, search filtering, and causality analysis.

## Background
Schema ambiguity will invalidate downstream indexing and replay. This bead establishes the ground-truth contract.

## Scope
- Define event types (ingress text, egress output, control markers, lifecycle markers).
- Define required metadata (pane/session/workflow correlation IDs, timestamps, sequence numbers, source).
- Define schema evolution/versioning rules and compatibility tests.

## Deliverables
- Recorder event schema spec and serialization contract.
- JSON-schema or equivalent testable schema artifact.

## Acceptance criteria
- Schema is precise enough for independent producer/consumer implementation.
- Evolution rules cover additive and breaking changes explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:46.35793-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:46.35793-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented recorder event schema types in recording.rs: RecorderEvent, RecorderEventPayload (4 variants), RecorderEventSource, RecorderEventCausality, 6 supporting enums, RECORDER_EVENT_SCHEMA_VERSION_V1 constant, parse_recorder_event_json() with version validation. All 5 schema contract tests pass. JSON schema at docs/flight-recorder/ft-recorder-event-v1.json verified.","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","title":"Implement ingress tap points for all mux-bound text/action injections","description":"## Goal
Implement ingress capture tap points (text/actions entering mux/panes) with minimal overhead and explicit source attribution.

## Background
Ingress visibility is required for full forensic replay and for understanding operator/agent actions leading to outcomes.

## Scope
- Identify ingress boundaries and hook locations.
- Capture payload + metadata in canonical schema.
- Ensure capture path does not break policy gating behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ingress capture implementation plan + instrumentation tests.
- Validation that policy decisions and ingress logs remain consistent.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ingress events are captured for all supported injection paths.
- Overhead budget for ingress capture is documented and met in benchmarks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:45:26.001155-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:45:26.001155-05:00","close_reason":"Ingress tap integrated into PolicyGatedInjector with 8 integration tests","labels":["capture","ingress","mux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864528Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.2.3","title":"Implement egress tap points with contiguous segment + explicit gap semantics","description":"## Goal
Implement egress capture tap points (pane/mux output leaving runtime paths) with contiguous segment semantics and explicit gap markers.

## Background
Egress capture is the primary data source for replay and search; it must preserve ordering and discontinuity semantics.

## Scope
- Hook egress at the authoritative output pipeline.
- Emit explicit discontinuity markers where continuity cannot be guaranteed.
- Preserve compatibility with existing ingest/pattern detection semantics.

## Deliverables
- Egress capture instrumentation with segment/gap semantics.
- Regression tests for continuity/gap behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- Egress events are complete for observed panes.
- Gap semantics are explicit and machine-detectable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:10:23.179118-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:10:23.179118-05:00","close_reason":"Egress tap integrated into TailerSupervisor with 7 passing integration tests","labels":["capture","egress","ingest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.894294Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
